entry.title: Re: Are there step by step guidelines in learning HUB for...
entry.content:
Hi @yuvarajtsiva 
Please check if the following learning material suits your inquiry:

  • Becoming an SAP BTP Solution Architect unit Exploring SAP BTP Guidance, Content and More
  • Developing with SAP Integration Suite 
    This learning journey is designed for integration designers and developers with responsibilities for building integration from and to cloud applications.
    After completing this learning journey, you will be able to create an API and use the required tools, processes, and operations in SAP Integration Suite. You will also be equipped with the skills to manage APIs including Cloud Integration (Integration Flows), and to model processes.

 Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Provisioning of Business Data Cloud : SAP Datasphere
entry.content: @SreekanthSurampally Unfortunately, you can't .

entry.title: MAG using Pre/post sorting of DESADV EDIFACT generated from SOAP TransportationOrderGenericRequest
entry.content:
Hi Everyone!

In this blog, we will go through the steps to create Mapping guidelines(MAG) for Delivery Schedule sent as a SOAP message.

The scenario being described here is: SAP S/4HANA Cloud SOAP TransportationOrderGenericRequest -> VDA EDIFACT DESADV

                                                                Figure 1. High level overview of transformation steps involved

 

 

Business Use Case: Automated Generation of VDA EDIFACT DESADV Using TransportationOrderGenericRequest

Problem statement: In TransportationOrderGenericRequest SOAP message, items are categorised using different IDs and hierarchical parent-child relationships. But these items are at the same level irrespective of the category, hence the challenge is to group related items in a hierarchical structure to generate a sorted EDI message.

Target structure(VDA EDIFACT) in a similar way has no hierarchy, so sorting of elements is needed to form groups of related elements is needed.

Such transformation, unfortunately, cannot be realised using standard MAG and some additional processing steps (pre- and post- processing and Pretransformation) are needed as described in following section.

Background:

A logistics company manages the transportation of goods for various manufacturers. The company is responsible for ensuring that shipments are delivered accurately and on time. To facilitate these operations, the company plans to automate the generation of VDA EDIFACT DESADV messages using standardized transportation order requests.

Following section will cover the logic for ID and ParentItemID for these Item elements.

Hierarchy Explanation

Item 1 is a top-level item (Shipment) with ID 1000000.

Item 2(outer package) with ID 000000007 is a child of Item 1 with ParentItemID 1000000.

Item 3(inner package) with ID 000000008 is a child of Item 2 with ParentItemID 000000007.

So, the hierarchy can be visualized as:

 

Item 1000000 (Shipment)

    └── Item 000000007 (Outer Package)

            └── Item 000000008 (Inner Package)

                   └── Item 000001000 (Product)

Or

Item 1000000 (Shipment)

    └── Item 000000010 (Package)

            └── Item 000000020 (Product)

This hierarchical relationship indicates nested structures, where one item contains or is a part of another, building a parent-child relationship within these categories.

Item Structure

Here is an outline of the relationships and logic behind ID and ParentItemID:

Item 1

 

<Item>
    <ID>1000000</ID>
    <CategoryCode>SHP</CategoryCode>

 

where

ID is a unique identifier for the item. Here, ID is 1000000.

CategoryCode: Indicates the type of item, in this case, SHP (Shipment).

Item 2

 

<Item>
    <ID>000000007</ID>
    <CategoryCode>PKG</CategoryCode>
    <Description>3</Description>
    <HierarchyRelationship>
        <ParentItemID>1000000</ParentItemID>
    </HierarchyRelationship>
    <Package>
        <ID>UN123456789</ID>
        <StandardPackageIDIndicator>true</StandardPackageIDIndicator>
        <PackagingMaterialInternalID>ABC-12345</PackagingMaterialInternalID>
        <RecipientPackagingMaterialInternalID>ABC-54321</RecipientPackagingMaterialInternalID>
    </Package>
</Item>

 

 where

ID is a unique identifier for the item. Here, ID is 000000007.

CategoryCode: Indicates the type of item, in this case, PKG (Package).

ParentItemID: It references the parent item's ID. Here, ParentItemID is 1000000, linking this item to the parent 1000000(SHP). This is referred in this document as Outer package.

Item 3

 

<Item>
    <ID>000000008</ID>
    <CategoryCode>PKG</CategoryCode>
    <HierarchyRelationship>
        <ParentItemID>000000007</ParentItemID>
    </HierarchyRelationship>
    <Package>
        <ID>UN987654321</ID>
        <StandardPackageIDIndicator>true</StandardPackageIDIndicator>
        <TypeCode>A001</TypeCode>
        <PackagingMaterialInternalID>ABC-54321</PackagingMaterialInternalID>
        <RecipientPackagingMaterialInternalID>ABC-54321</RecipientPackagingMaterialInternalID>
    </Package>
</Item>

 

MIG qualification

To associate contained packages and products in a shipment, flat structure with all handling units (shipment, package and product) which are at the same level in SOAP message need to be converted to a hierarchical structure.

As a first step to generate this hierarchical structure, Items in MIG for SOAP message are qualified as SHP (for Shipment), OUT (for outer package), PKG (for inner package) and PRD (for Product) with a custom Codelist.

MIG qualified using codelist created above

 

Pre-processing

To differentiate between an outer package and inner package, pre-processing step is used which replaces CategoryCode code of outer package from PKG to OUT.

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<xsl:stylesheet xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform" version="1.0">
    <xsl:output method="xml" indent="yes"/>
    <xsl:strip-space elements="*"/>

    <!-- Identity transformation to copy all elements and attributes -->
    <xsl:template match="@* | node()">
        <xsl:copy>
            <xsl:apply-templates select="@* | node()"/>
        </xsl:copy>
    </xsl:template>

    <!-- Template to modify the CategoryCode -->
    <xsl:template
        match="//Item[HierarchyRelationship/ParentItemID = ..//Item[CategoryCode = 'SHP']/ID and ID = ..//Item[CategoryCode = 'PKG']/HierarchyRelationship/ParentItemID]/CategoryCode">
        <CategoryCode>OUT</CategoryCode>

    </xsl:template>

    <xsl:template
        match="//Item[HierarchyRelationship/ParentItemID = ..//Item[CategoryCode = 'SHP']/ID and not(ID = ..//Item[CategoryCode = 'PKG']/HierarchyRelationship/ParentItemID)]">
        <Item>
            <ID>
                <xsl:value-of select="..//Item[CategoryCode = 'SHP']/ID"/>
            </ID>
            <CategoryCode>OUT</CategoryCode>
            <HierarchyRelationship>
                <ParentItemID>
                    <xsl:value-of select="..//Item[CategoryCode = 'SHP']/ID"/>
                </ParentItemID>
            </HierarchyRelationship>
        </Item>
        <xsl:copy-of select="."/>
    </xsl:template>
</xsl:stylesheet>

 

The stylesheet performs the following transformations:

  • Copies all elements and attributes unchanged using an identity transformation.
  • Modifies CategoryCode to OUT for specific Item elements that meet certain hierarchical conditions.
  • Creates a new Item element with a modified CategoryCode and hierarchical relationship in certain conditions where the ID does not match a parent item's ID in a packaging context.
  • This ensures items that are related in specific ways within the hierarchy have their CategoryCode updated appropriately, and new items added if necessary

Pretransformation

Generating hierarchical structure using Pretransformation:

Documentation

Linking PKG -> PRD

 

Linking OUT -> PKG

 

Pretransformation step will generate following message structure

Item [CategoryCode = OUT]

    └── Item_gq_PKG_Copy

            └── Item_gq_PRD_Copy

Since we create a dummy OUT segment in pre-processing, we skip generating SG11 segment for this dummy node using following condition in main transformation.

 

if ($nodes_in/outerPackageID = $nodes_in/shipmentID)
  then false()
else true()

 

Main transformation

In main transformation, two SG11 segments (outer package and inner package) are generated.

As VDA EDIFACT file has no hierarchical structure, EDI elements need to be sorted to group relevant handling units together.

To achieve this, a unique key is generated which will be used in post-processing to sort EDI elements.

Outer package

 

<xsl:sequence select="concat('2;', $nodes_in/ParentID[1])"/>

 

Inner package

 

<xsl:sequence select="concat('2;', $nodes_in/ParentID[1], ';', $nodes_in/ItemID[1])"/>

 

Post-processing

The unique key (combination of current ID and parent ID) generated earlier will be used to sort EDI elements

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!-- ============================================================================================= -->
<!-- This script sorts the Consignment Packages Group (SG11) according to the request hierarchy, and -->
<!-- sets the correct ordinal numbers in DE 7164 (hierachical ids ) as well in DE 7166             -->
<!-- (hierarchical parent ids).                                                                    -->
<!--                                                                                               -->
<!-- ============================================================================================= -->

<xsl:stylesheet xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform"
    xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"
    exclude-result-prefixes="xsl xs"
    version="2.0">
    <xsl:output method="xml" indent="yes"/>
    <!--<xsl:strip-space elements="*"/>-->
    <!-- ============================================================================================= -->
    <!-- Template Match: Root node -->
    <!-- ============================================================================================= -->   
    <xsl:template match="/node()">
        <xsl:copy>
            <xsl:apply-templates select="node()"/>
        </xsl:copy>
    </xsl:template>
    
    <!-- ============================================================================================= -->
    <!-- Template match: Produce whole message including the sorted structure of SG11 segments  -->
    <!-- ============================================================================================= -->
    <xsl:template match="M_DESADV">
        <xsl:copy>
            <xsl:copy-of select="S_UNH | S_BGM | S_DTM | G_SG1 | G_SG2 | S_MEA | G_SG5 | G_SG6"/>
            <xsl:call-template name="ctSG11Sort"/>
            <xsl:copy-of select="S_CNT | S_UNT"/>
        </xsl:copy>
    </xsl:template>
    
    <!-- ============================================================================================= -->
    <!-- Call Template: Sort structure  -->
    <!-- ============================================================================================= -->
    <xsl:template name="ctSG11Sort">
        <xsl:variable name="vSortedOutput">
            <xsl:for-each-group select="G_SG11" group-by="tokenize(./S_CPS/D_7166, ';')[2]">
                <xsl:apply-templates select="current-group()[./S_CPS/D_7164 = '2']"/>
                <xsl:apply-templates select="current-group()[./S_CPS/D_7164 = '1']"/>
                
                
            </xsl:for-each-group>
        </xsl:variable>
        <xsl:variable name="vSetItemID">
            <xsl:apply-templates select="$vSortedOutput" mode="item"/>
        </xsl:variable>
        <xsl:apply-templates select="$vSetItemID" mode="parent"/>
    </xsl:template>
    
    <!-- ============================================================================================= -->
    <!-- Template match: Set item ID -->
    <!-- ============================================================================================= -->
    <xsl:template match="D_7164" mode="item">
        <xsl:variable name="vID" select="count(preceding::D_7164) + 1"/>
        <xsl:copy>
            <xsl:value-of select="$vID"/>            
        </xsl:copy>        
    </xsl:template>
    <xsl:template match="D_7166" mode="item">
        <xsl:variable name="vID" select="count(preceding::D_7166) + 1"/>
        <xsl:copy>
            <xsl:value-of select="concat(., ';', $vID)"/>            
        </xsl:copy>        
    </xsl:template>
    <xsl:template match="node()" mode="item">
        <xsl:copy>
            <xsl:apply-templates select="node()" mode="item"/>
        </xsl:copy>
    </xsl:template>    
    
    <!-- ============================================================================================= -->
    <!-- Template match: Set parent ID -->
    <!-- ============================================================================================= -->
    <xsl:template match="D_7166" mode="parent">
        <xsl:variable name="vIndex" select="tokenize(current(),';')"/>
        <xsl:variable name="vIndexCount" select="count($vIndex)"/>
        <xsl:variable name="vParentIDArray" select="preceding::D_7166[count(tokenize(.,';')) = 1]"/>
        <xsl:variable name="vParentID" select="for $i in $vParentIDArray return if(contains ($i, $vIndex[2])) then $i else ()"/>
        <xsl:choose>
            <xsl:when test="$vIndex[2] = '#NA#'"/>
            <xsl:when test="count($vIndex) = 3">
                <xsl:copy>
                    <xsl:value-of select="$vIndex[1]"/>
                </xsl:copy>                                
            </xsl:when>
            <xsl:otherwise>
                <xsl:copy>
                    <xsl:value-of select="tokenize($vParentID, ';')[last()]"/>
                </xsl:copy>                
            </xsl:otherwise>
        </xsl:choose>
        <xsl:if test="$vIndex[2] != '#NA#'">
        </xsl:if>
    </xsl:template>
    <xsl:template match="node()" mode="parent">
        <xsl:copy>
            <xsl:apply-templates select="node()" mode="parent"/>
        </xsl:copy>
    </xsl:template>
    
    <!-- ============================================================================================= -->
    <!-- Template match: Process remaining nodes -->
    <!-- ============================================================================================= -->
    <xsl:template match="node()">
        <xsl:copy>
            <xsl:apply-templates select="node()"/>
        </xsl:copy>
    </xsl:template>
    
</xsl:stylesheet>

 

The post-processing XSLT script aims to reorder and assign hierarchical IDs to the Consignment Packages Group (SG11) within the M_DESADV node.

Conclusion

We have seen that using features like pre-processing, pretransformation and post-processing, we can transform the message structures to group related elements which makes mapping easier and helps to avoid complex custom sorting functions.

entry.title: Re: NEW! Introduction to Artificial Intelligence and SAP Business AI
entry.content:
Here is an article about Team Liquid online sporting and the power of AI. It pulls together the idea of AI powered by Joule to provide the teams with almost real time, powerful analytics.  Students may find it to be a fun way to learn. 

Team Liquid article link

entry.title: Re: I've shared a story report to a manager but when the...
entry.content: If you see some of the data but not Personal information of the employee it could be that the manager is missing  Manage User > Data Access: User

entry.title: Understanding the distinction between SAP Asset Manager and SAP Asset Management
entry.content:
To make the most of SAP’s Asset Management solutions, it’s important to understand the distinction between SAP Asset Manager and SAP Asset Management, as this ensures your support cases are directed efficiently and handled by the most relevant experts.

SAP Asset Manager is a mobile application designed to support work orders, notifications, condition monitoring, material consumption, time management, and failure analysis. In contrast, SAP Asset Management refers to the broader asset management capabilities embedded within SAP S/4HANA and other core modules. While the names are similar, these are two distinct areas managed by different support teams.

We understand that the similarity in names between SAP Asset Manager and SAP Asset Management can sometimes create confusion when submitting support cases. To help streamline your experience, it's important to select the correct component so your case reaches the most relevant team without delay. To assist you with this, there is detailed Knowledge Base Article for your reference: KBA 3613810 - Mobile application for SAP Asset Management - SAP for Me

This KBA explains how to select the correct component when submitting a case for SAP Asset Manager. It also outlines the key product information and guidance you’ll need to get your case to the right support experts from the beginning.

As a best practice, always ensure that you're referencing SAP Asset Manager as the product when the issue pertains to the mobile app. This includes scenarios involving mobile UI issues, synchronization problems, mobile-specific performance concerns, or mobile offline capabilities.

If you're unsure which component to choose, the KBA provides clear examples and screenshots to guide you through the process.

Additionally, we recommend consulting the official help documentation for SAP Intelligent Asset Management, which provides context on how SAP Asset Manager fits within the broader asset management solution landscape: SAP Help Portal – SAP Intelligent Asset Management

This guide outlines the architecture, supported features, integration points, and use cases for SAP Asset Manager and related solutions. We highly encourage all customers working with SAP Asset Manager to familiarize themselves with KBA 3613810 and use it as a reference when opening new cases.

Thank you for helping us support you more efficiently.

entry.title: Re: Couldn't create an instance of service 'ABAP environm...
entry.content:
Hi Paul,

Thanks, it works now.

What was the problem?

entry.title: Activity-based consolidation of investments (C/I) postings split by profit center
entry.content:
Dear GR Community,

I have a question related to activity-based consolidation of investments (C/I) postings. We have provided activity type 05 for the retained earnings amount in the Additional C/I Control Data File, split by profit center. For example:

 

Conso UnitFS ItemAmountSubitemSub CatProfitCenterInvest Act Type
A12000100,209151AA05
A12000120,609151AB05
A1200075,809151AC05

The expected posting is a reclassification from FS item 12000 (Retained Earnings) to FS item 11310 (Consolidated Reserves).

The total reclassified amount using document type 3B is correct, but the profit center split is missing. Here's an example of the actual posting:

 

FS ItemAmountSubitemSub CatProfitCenter
12000-100,209151AA
12000-120,609151AB
12000-75,809151AC
$12000100,209151AA
$12000120,609151AB
$1200075,809151AC
11310296,609151 

Is there any way to ensure that the posting to FS item 11310 also considers the profit center split?

We are using Group Reporting on-premise 2021 — could this be a limitation of our version? Has it been addressed in later versions?

Thank you for your support.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: FTE and Proration examples in Compensation
entry.content:
hi @catlin 

Yes but for Time Off if you want to distinguish between Time Entries in the proration you would need to add a new field in EC as described here: https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/time-off-integration-in-compensation-eligibility-rules/ba-p/13579851

 

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: paging in RAP service - Data is getting copied from 2nd page to first page
entry.content: no one can help with this infomration only.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Adaptation Transport Organizer
entry.content:
Hi @Jocelyn_Dart : I added one Item text field in the standard app (F1612)  but I am not seeing the TR showing up when clicking on "Publish" button in Adapt UI scenario.

Also post saving a new app variant I am getting below error message.

Error

Sorry, a technical error occurred. Please try again later, or contact your administrator. Reason: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'getId') at S._getRelevantOverlaysOnEditableChange

Please let me know your suggestions/comments. Our version is S4HANA 2021 FPS02.

SAPUI5 v 1.96.27

Regards,

Bharanidharan S P M

entry.title: Re: Migration Cockpit (LTMC) is the new black
entry.content:
Hi,

really nice blog. One question regarding your statement:

" processing logs are from another century ... it is like LTMC is not meant to display, navigate, analyze logs ..."

Are you talking about the new Migration Cockpit app delivered since 2020 release or the old deprecated web-dynpro based transaction LTMC? For the LTMC I agree with the statement that processing logs are from another century.

But the new migration cockpit app has a much more better message log and anaylizing capacity and in combination with the data migration status app available with 2023 release, you will have additional capabilities to analyze migrated data or do audits on migrated data.

BR

Frank

entry.title: Re: Failed to get service instance from ADT
entry.content: Did you get any solution? I am facing the same problem 11/jun/2025

entry.title: Re: how to extract a portion of the string from the given...
entry.content:
Hello,

If you are looking for the second word in a given string then you can try the following code:

def String getSecondValue(String input, String delimiter) {
    String[] tab = input.split(delimiter)
    if (tab.size() >= 2) {
        return tab[1]
    } else {
        return ""
    }
}

BR,

Andrzej

entry.content:
Hi @praveenkumarparsha 

Were you able to resolve this issue and add XML start and end tags?
I am facing the same issue and looking for a solution.

Regards,
Sakshi

entry.title: WHERE CAN I FIND THE LATEST UPDATES TO SAP
entry.content: I recently noticed that I now need to add the BL / tracking number when booking a goods receipt. How do I find out when updates are to be made?

entry.title: Automate Business Partner Updates with SAP Build
entry.content:
Update Business Partner Bank details in minutes — not days

Maintaining accurate Business Partner (BP) master data, especially bank details, is crucial yet challenging for businesses. Requests to update bank information traditionally involve manual validation, document verification, and data entry, leading to delays, errors, and increased costs.

With SAP Build Process Automation integrated with SAP Document Information Extraction (DOX) and generative AI capabilities, you can automate these processes, dramatically improving accuracy and response times. This blog demonstrates how your organization could automatically process Business Partner bank detail updates, ensuring rapid, error-free transactions while reserving manual review for exceptional cases.

Why Automation Matters

Bank detail updates for Business Partners—whether suppliers, citizens, employees or even customers—often entail cumbersome manual processes:

  • Collection of supporting documentation (bank certificate, identification documents).

  • Manual validation of names, bank details, and document authenticity.

  • Re-keying data, prone to human error.

  • Lengthy approval chains causing delays.

These tasks consume significant human resources and cause operational delays, financial risks, and compliance issues. Each manual intervention represents an additional cost, a source of potential errors, and delayed response times, negatively impacting business agility and stakeholder satisfaction.

Automated Process with SAP Build – How it Works

The SAP Build process, combined with Document Information Extraction and generative AI, offers a streamlined, automated approach:

  1. Request Submission: Users complete a form with their name, bank details, and upload required documents (bank certificate and identification). The form is surfaced in SAP Build Work Zone and uploaded files land instantly in SAP Document Management Service (DMS) for secure storage.

  2. Automated Parallel Document Processing:

    • Bank Certificate: Document Information Extraction Premium leverages generative AI for flexible extraction across different bank document formats.

    • Identification Document (National ID/DNI): Document Information Extraction Basic extracts standard data quickly and reliably.

  3. Verification and Validation:

    • The bank certificate undergoes an additional step for automated bank certificate’s digital signature verification, ensuring document authenticity.

    • The extracted data from documents and the initial form are compared automatically for consistency (e.g., exact match between names, account numbers).

  4. Conditional Rule:

    • If data matches precisely and verification succeeds, SAP Build automatically updates the Business Partner bank details in SAP S/4HANA.

    • If discrepancies are identified (such as a name mismatch), the request is routed to a human reviewer for manual approval or rejection through an approval form.

  5. Notification & Audit:

    • Automatic email notifications inform the requester of the approval status.

    • All processing steps, decisions, and document validations are archived for auditing purposes.


Benefits Achieved

  • Significant reduction in manual work, decreasing operational costs.

  • Faster processing time, increasing business agility.

  • Enhanced accuracy, reducing risk and ensuring compliance.

  • Improved team productivity, redirecting human resources to strategic tasks.



SAP BTP Components - Architecture

Below is the technical architecture used for this automated solution:

 This is just an illustrative example; your actual process and system integrations might differ depending on specific business requirements.



Conclusion & Next Steps

Automating Business Partner bank detail updates not only saves considerable time and cost but also enhances accuracy and compliance. With SAP Build and SAP BTP, you can automate critical processes easily, freeing your teams to focus on strategic tasks.

Start your automation journey today and experience the benefits firsthand.

entry.title: Re: Atualização data rejeição NFe em Homologação
entry.content:
Bom dia, 

é uma ótima notícia e alívio para os clientes SAP que estão com diversos projetos em andamento, ao menos até Outubro/2025.

Apenas uma correção,  a SAP não está planejando entregar notas e soluções de Reforma Tributária para o SAP GRC  (SAP NFE), mas apenas para o SAP DRC, conforme o regulation Change Manager (https://app.main.regulatory-change-manager.cloud.sap/index.html#/detail/GSREQAMER-7112).

entry.title: Re: Generation repository objects ADT
entry.content:
Please put the below fields into the table and then generate the repository objects.

last_changed_at : abp_lastchange_tstmpl;

created_by : abp_creation_user;

created_at : abp_creation_tstmpl;

last_changed_by : abp_lastchange_user;

local_last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;

entry.title: Re: WalkMe Realize 2025: A Personalized, Power-Packed Experience for Changemakers
entry.content: So nice to see this post. Welcome to the Business Transformation group! @Mary_Greening 🙂

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2505
entry.content:
Since the rollout of 2505, Joule for ABAP does not seem to work correctly anymore. Chat requests to joule are answered with "An unexpected error occured: An exception was raised" or in case of unit test generation ""An unexpected error occured Intelligent scenario AUI response error" 

Does anybody experience the same issues?

entry.title: Job Requisition failed requisition operator not found
entry.content:
Job requisition failed when trying to create a job requisition from retail template. Following message appears 

"Creation of a job requisition for position xxxx failed. Details: Cannot find operator T (JOBREQ_FIELD_COORDINATOR) in template 1541 with the index 0 "

entry.title: My Intro
entry.content: Hi Everyone, this is Prakhar and I am working as SAP BTP developer over the past few years. Very Happy to join this group and looking forward to connecting with you on technical discussions.

entry.title: IAS/IPS – Efficient Group Setup?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We are using SAP IAS/IPS with Azure as Idp. We have already connected several SAP Cloud Apps to IAS, and more apps will be added.

Access to these apps is controlled by groups in Azure. For example, depending on a user’s role in SAC or Enable Now, different groups are created in Azure.

In SAC, we already have at least 10 groups, and in Enable Now even more. As more SAP Cloud Apps are connected, the number of Azure groups increases quickly.

My question is:
Is there a better or easier way to manage roles and access without creating so many groups in Azure?

How do you manage this in your environment to keep it organized and scalable?

Thanks for your help

Best regards

entry.title: Re: "Hands on Practice Undefined" message for practice sy...
entry.content:
Hi @pink_submarine 

If you run into issue during enrollment or practice in the learning system please create a case in our  ticketing system.
Please make sure to select "Practice Systems" as the “Service Category” and kindly fill out the appropriate form.
When raising an inquiry, include detailed information in the description to help our team assist you better.
Please also check our Practice systems support site where you find a list of general questions and answers on practice systems, all designed to help you to resolve issues may encounter on SAP Learning.

Kind regards
Margit

 

Kind regards

Margit

entry.title: Re: Introducing the new pipeline concept in Cloud Integration
entry.content:
@sagargirijan storing the payload in a property is a common approach for use cases where a mapping or similar will overwrite the original payload, at least better than storing in a datastore, but I can confirm that we have run performance tests with larger payloads, as far as I can recall up to the size of 1MB.

Alex

entry.title: Unlocking Data Potential: What I Learned from SAP Sapphire
entry.content:
Unlocking Data Potential: What I Learned from SAP Sapphire

Hi everyone! I’m Kara Reed, Senior Director of Product Marketing at SAP. At this year’s SAP Sapphire, I had the privilege of co-presenting a session with Brian Suk from Databricks on a topic that’s very close to my heart: how organizations can unlock the full value of their data through the combined power of SAP Business Data Cloud and Databricks.

Our session—" SAP and Databricks open a bold new era of data and AI"—highlighted how businesses can build trusted, AI-powered applications with SAP Databricks, a fully SAP-managed solution that combines industry leading AI/ML, data science, and data engineering capabilities with semantically rich, business-ready data from SAP applications. Let me take you behind the scenes and share a few insights from the session—and what I took away from the event overall.

Driving Value with Data + AI

Our goal was to help attendees answer three key questions:

  • Why SAP Business Data Cloud and Databricks?
  • What value can this bring to my organization?
  • How do they work together in practice?

It’s no secret that data and AI are reshaping the future of business. Companies that can turn their data into actionable insights—quickly, securely, and at scale—are the ones that will lead in their industries. But too many are still held back by siloed data, inconsistent quality, and tools that don’t talk to each other.

The Trust Gap is Real

One of the most striking stats we shared was from a recent survey of 1,200 business and IT leaders: only 34% have high trust in their data capabilities. That’s a wake-up call. Organizations are investing heavily in data platforms, yet many still struggle with aligning IT and business needs or ensuring data is ready for AI-driven use cases.

What SAP and Databricks Are Doing Differently

What stood out most in our session was showing how SAP and Databricks are partnering to change that. This is more than just a technical integration—it’s a strategic shift.

With SAP Business Data Cloud, customers can:

  • Access live, trusted SAP data products without duplication
  • Preserve business semantics and lineage
  • ML/AI development in pro-code environment

SAP Databricks in Business Data Cloud enables a zero-copy architecture that reduces time, effort, and risk when building production-grade AI solutions.

What Customers Told Me

One of my favorite parts of SAP Sapphire was speaking with customers one-on-one. Many were excited about how SAP-managed data products could drastically reduce the time and complexity of their data engineering tasks. The idea of getting "AI-ready" data out of SAP systems, without writing hundreds of custom pipelines, really resonated.

Several clients mentioned how this approach could help close the gap between IT and business teams, giving analysts and data scientists quicker access to meaningful, governed data. That’s a huge win for productivity and innovation.

My Takeaway

SAP Sapphire reminded me that we’re at a tipping point: data and AI are no longer "nice to have"—they’re critical to staying competitive. But what makes the difference isn’t just technology. It’s trust. Trust in the data. Trust in the platform. And trust that the tools will work together.

SAP Business Data Cloud and Databricks are helping organizations make that leap.

Let’s Keep the Conversation Going

One of the best parts of the event? Meeting new people—partners, customers, colleagues—and hearing how you're tackling data and AI challenges in the real world. Let’s not stop here.

👉 Introduce yourself in the comments! I’d love to hear what you're working on and how you see data + AI evolving at your organization.

And if you're looking to go deeper, here are some helpful resources:

  • Try it out!
  • Business Data Cloud help documentation

Let’s keep learning from each other.

Related community managed tags you should subscribe to remain informed about this topic:   

 Data and Analytics SAP Datasphere SAP Business Data Cloud

    

Related community topic pages:   

Data and Analytics  SAP Business Data Cloud  SAP Datasphere

entry.title: Re: Project Glow: New Design Coming to SAP Community on Monday, June 2
entry.content:
@ASAPGUI - we have checked on our end, and the table interaction is not something that was introduced with the redesign, but rather that two code sample blocks are presented in two columns in the same table. 
This will be fixed when we release the full-width reading view for blog posts. (currently scheduled for our next release)

entry.title: How to Send Automated GR Email Notifications to Buyers in SAP Using Output Determination
entry.content:
In many organisations, the buyer or procurement team must be notified when a goods receipt (GR) is posted for a Purchase Order (PO). This ensures prompt actions such as invoice verification, quality checks, or inventory updates. SAP provides a standard functionality that uses output determination to automate notifications via system-generated messages.

In this blog, we will walk through the steps to configure an automatic GR message notification to the buyer's inbox (SAP Business Workplace) when a goods receipt is posted in a specific plant and storage location.

🏢 Business Scenario

Imagine a manufacturing company that receives raw materials at multiple plants. The procurement team responsible for Plant 1000 and Storage Location 1004 wants to be alerted as soon as goods are received at this location so they can initiate inspection and payment processes quickly.

To automate this, we can configure a GR Message output type (MLGR) that triggers a system mail to the buyer whenever a GR is posted at that specific plant/storage location.

Step 1: Create Condition Table

Path: SPRO → Materials Management → Inventory Management and Physical Inventory → Output Determination → Maintain Condition Tables → Output: Create Conditions Table

Create a new condition table (e.g., 914) that includes key fields such as Plant and Storage Location.

Step 2: Maintain Access Sequence

Path: SPRO → Materials Management → Inventory Management and Physical Inventory → Output Determination → Maintain Access Sequences

Assign the newly created condition table (914) to access sequence 0002 (used for GR emails).

Step 3: Maintain Output Type

Path: SPRO → Materials Management → Inventory Management and Physical Inventory → Output Determination → Maintain Output Types

 

  • Use or customise the MLGR output type.

  • Navigate to Mail title and Texts to define the subject and message body for the GR message.

Navigate to mail title and texts

Step 4: Maintain Output Determination Procedure

Path: SPRO → Materials Management → Inventory Management and Physical Inventory → Output Determination → Maintain Output Determination Procedures

Ensure Requirement 171 is assigned (this requirement is specific to GR email functionality)

Step 5: Maintain Condition Record (Transaction: MN21)

 

  • Enter the condition type MLGR.

  • Provide plant and storage location values.

  • On the Communication tab, enter the SAP user ID of the buyer.

  • Select Express Mail if instant pop-up notification is required.

Note: You may get a pop-up assigning a dummy recipient—click Yes to proceed, when clicked on Communication.

Step 6: Create Purchase Order (Transaction: ME21N)

 

  • In the PO Header, navigate to the Delivery/Invoice tab.

  • Ensure the checkbox GR Message is selected.

Step 7: Perform Goods Receipt (Transaction: MIGO)

 

  • Post the GR using MIGO for the PO.

  • Make sure the storage location matches the one configured in the condition record.

Step 8: Check Buyer Inbox (Transaction: SBWP)

 

  • Go to SBWP (SAP Business Workplace).

  • The buyer should see a new message containing the GR details.

Since Note was maintained in step 5, it will appear here. 

Step 9: Option: Review via SOST (Mail Log)

 

  • Execute transaction SOST to check the email/message log.

  • Click on Choose to view the actual message content sent to the buyer.

If you found this guide helpful, don’t miss my other SAP MM blogs covering real-world automation, configuration tips, and best practices for procurement and inventory management.

Click to view other SAP MM Blogs - SAP Community

 

Regards,

Leelamohan Mannem

entry.title: Re: Download course material from SAP Learning
entry.content:
Hi @NatalieVR 
The embedded, downloadable course PDF is currently not a supported feature on 
SAP Learning at the moment. It has been requested as a future roadmap consideration.
Kind regards
Margit

I need to know where to start in my journey to gaining knowledge (training) to prepare for the exam within the next 3-4 months. Finally, I'm not technical but functional;-). 

Thank you in advance, Tina

entry.title: How to create a Dropdown List Variable to prefilter an SAPB1 User Query?
entry.content:
Hi all. I'm trying to figure out if it's possible to create a user query that uses a predefined list of options/values, which dependent on the option picked, will filter the query data on output.

To give a basic example, the OITM table and its ItemCode and OnHand fields. On execution, I want the Selection Criteria to provide a single input, being a dropdown list of the strings "ALL", "POS", "NEG", and "ZERO". Depending on which option is picked by the user, the returning query data would be the ItemCode and OnHand columns for items where the OnHand value is > 0, < 0, or = 0 (with "ALL" applying no filtering to the data).

I know that this can be done within the query preview using the filter table option, but I want to implement a preset filtering in the query from the start, as to potentially reduce the number of returning rows so the query doesn't take as long to generate.

This here was my initial attempt based on the resources I could find about this topic, but it didn't work (and I feel like the '/Dropdown/' syntax isn't correct here either):

SELECT T0.ItemCode, T0.OnHand
FROM OITM T0
WHERE
([%0/Dropdown/ALL,POS,NEG,ZERO] = 'ALL')
OR ([%0] = 'POS' AND T0.OnHand > 0)
OR ([%0] = 'NEG' AND T0.OnHand < 0)
OR ([%0] = 'ZERO' AND T0.OnHand = 0)

Any help here would be highly appreciative. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Rehire scenario with EC mini master
entry.content: Hi, Rehire check never triggers, do you mean during Review New Hire Data review? Can you pls elaborate the solution.

entry.title: How to filter a cds search help result based on a UI field?
entry.content:
@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [

{ entity:{ element: 'Version',

name: 'ZPSBC_ITEMX_SH' } },

{ distinctValues: true, useForValidation: true },

{ additionalBinding: [{ localElement: 'Aadhar',

element: 'AadharID',

usage: #FILTER }] }

 

I am using the highlighted annotation in bold to filter the cds result.

I want the result of the cds to return values of version where aadhar value

matches not all unrelated values. but it is not working. Please help.

 

regards,

Prasenjit

entry.title: Transaction ME6H does not display my invoice criteria and the criterion I created
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I would like to know if it is possible in SAP ECC to display,
through transaction ME6H for a given period, my manual sub criterion that I created as well as the two invoice sub-criteria that exist by default.
When I run my transaction ME6H, it displays all existing criteria except the sub criteria invoices and my sub criterion that I created. I have activated supplier invoice evaluation, and the scores are displayed correctly in ME63.

someone to help me?

But any time later, it gives me this error and I have to use manual capture. I tried with multiple tenants with no luck. Thanks for any suggestions.

SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Re: Items not appearing in library despite being uploaded...
entry.content: @sysfpathforward, the preview and production environments are gradually receiving the "Enablement status of near real-time search updates". More information can be found in KBA 3582342. Regardless of whether your impacted system has it enabled, it's expected that the course will be available in the enhanced search after 72h (KBA 3511612). For further analysis, a case with Support is required.

entry.title: Re: Custom Services UI feature in Sales Service Cloud v2

entry.title: Re: Add Custom Fields in S/4HANA Public Cloud Migration C...
entry.content:
Hi,

Document and Document Version are document info record fields. To add a document info record to a product, you must use the related data migration object for document info records: 

Document info record


https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/e0c077f8cd8c413abedacec4f71fc61f.html 

Inspection Text, Basic Data Text, Internal Comment are long text. To migrate Product long text you must use: 

Product - extend existing record with long text

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/6a317abf80aa4e7598b690bc394688d5.html 

Text Object Text ID

MATERIALBEST (Purchasing text)
MATERIALGRUN (Basic text)
MATERIALIVER (Internal note)
MATERIALPRUE (Inspection text)
MDTXTLTXT (MRP text)
MVKE0001 (Sales text)

In Public Cloud you can only use the Modeler App to add fields that are enabled to be enhanced by SAP.

entry.title: Re: Custom Services UI feature in Sales Service Cloud v2
entry.content: https://github.com/SAP-samples/sales-and-service-cloud-extensions/tree/main/CustomServiceBasicCAPSample

Hi  @William_Yu ,

entry.title: Re: Modern ABAP: Reduce# operator and FOR GROUPS
entry.content: Nice !

entry.title: Re: How to restrict access to view of supplier invoices i...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

If you’re working in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and want to stop users (like people on B2B contracts) from seeing each other’s supplier invoices in the Supplier Invoice List app even though they’re in the same Company Code and Purchasing Group  the key is to control access based on the Cost Center.

Right now, since they share the same company and purchasing group, they’ll normally be able to see all supplier invoices. But if each person is only responsible for their own Cost Center, we can use that as the restriction point.

 

Here’s how you can do it:

1. Create a custom business role
Go to the app called “Maintain Business Roles”
Find the role your users are using for example, SAP_BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT and copy it to create a custom one, like ZBR_AP_LIMITED.

 

2. Set up access restrictions
In the new custom role, go to the “Maintain Restrictions” tab.
Look for a restriction type called C_ACCTASGN – Account Assignment.
This lets you limit access based on things like Cost Center, Internal Order, etc.

    • Choose “Restricted”
    • Add the Cost Center(s) the user should be allowed to see
    • Leave all other cost centers out

This will make sure each person only sees invoices related to their own cost center not someone else’s.

  • Assign the custom role to each user
    Use the Maintain Business Users app to assign the right role to each person, with the matching cost center restriction.

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: DDIC Structure for Migration Cockpit Objects
entry.content:
Hello Viktorvk,

the sender structures S_* are no DDIC structures, they only exists as  entries within the migration workbench configuration tables.

The structures are then generated into the coding. But each sender structure of a migration project is available as temporary table on the SAP HANA database: 
If you are an on-premises or Private Cloud customer you will see that tables within SE11 per migration project. You need to have the three letter Mass transfer ID of your project. The naming convention of a structure is as follows:

see 2733253 - FAQ for SAP S/4HANA migration cockpit. Migration approach: Transfer / Migrate data using staging tables - SAP for Me

  • Below is an example for a generated staging table for object Work center (SIF_WORK_CNTR) and structure S_KAZY

  • Table name: /1LT/DS<sys-id><consecutive number>

    • For example: /1LT/DSQKX000928

  • Table description: Z_<Alias = technical Object name without SIF_>_<Mass transfer ID>:<structure name>

    • For example: Z_WORK_CNTR_U02:S_KAZY

Best is to search for then name via description.

The last two fields are not part of the sender structure, they are status flags for the data migration. Also the first field MANDT is not part of the defined sender structure

BR
Frank

entry.title: Re: Alternatives for class attendance registration
entry.content: @RafaelHernandez, there are open enhancement requests that'd match your scenario: link1, link2. Although these aren't currently available features, it's recommended reviewing them and voting if desired.

entry.title: Re: Job Requisition failed requisition operator not found
entry.content:
Hello!

Without knowing your configuratiuon and setup, that type of error could be pointing to a couple of different things that you can check:

Data mapping to populate the your Coordinator role (T). Field might be missing from the template entirely, or it's not correctly mapped to a corresponding value or field in the position data. Might need to check the position to requisition mapping rule as well.

Check the requisition template:

  • to examine the field mappings to see if it's correctly linked to the appropriate position data or employee data fields.If the
  • If the template is outdated or there were some recent changes and try updating it to the latest version in an attempt to repair it.

entry.title: Re: Atualização data rejeição NFe em Homologação
entry.content: Muito bom!

entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step Guide to Data Migration Cockpit in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Karthick

nice blog, one comment to your statement:

"The uploaded template file must be less than 100 MB in size. If your data is too large, consider splitting it into multiple files."

You can also use ZIP files. To avoid splitting in multiple files you can also try to use CSV files instead of the XML files. Using CSV files, you can easily tailor your files and only need to use the files for the structures and fields you want to process. A CSV file has much less administrative data than an XML file. The 100 MB limit is the same, but with tailored CSV files this http-protocol related size limit is not reached that fast as using XML files.

BR

Frank

entry.title: Re: Optional/Required field change via Migration Object M...
entry.content:
Hello Naresha,

that message ID MM 85 is thrown by the API used to migrate the data. That error message has nothing to do with the visibility definition of the migration template. Within the migration template you will only see fields being marked as mandatory that are always mandatory- Fields that can be maintained mandatory or not mandatory via business process configuration or fields that are dependent on field value combinations mandatory or not mandatory are never marked as mandatory within the migration template. Each sheet/structure of the migration template has an assigned database table. Setting a field Mandatory or not is done via database constraints. Changing database constraints dynamically is not possible. 

For the API the material group is a mandatory field. You must either verify if you can configure this field within the different business process settings for material master to be not mandatory or you must provide a value for that field. 
You can also check the KBA 2756835 - Material Group MARA-MATKL is mandatory for MM02 despite set as optional - SAP for Me
There it is explained that if data for purchasing view is being used, material group is always mandatory.

BR
Frank

entry.title: Re: Atualização data rejeição NFe em Homologação
entry.content:
Oi, Alan,

Obrigado pela observação e correção. É DRC mesmo e me atrapalhei aqui na revisão!

Vai nos ajudar bastante mesmo, principalmente a manter a pressão cardiáca e stress controlados.

Agora entendi o motivo da SAP ter rejeitado meu post no WorkZone da Reforma Tributária.

entry.title: ACR - MX LOCALIZATION - Missing Reporting Activities ID or Report Activity ID
entry.content:
Hi community 

We are trying to implement ACR for MX Localization

After BC set activation we notice there is missing "activity ID" from the FIORI side (even thought exist on the maintenance table SRFVC_RPG_ENT)

Therefore, We cannot generate the reports

How can input the missing entries?

If I choose "add" from the FIORI side it ask for a Z Custom Object, so I do not think is the right solution

Thanks in advance for your help

entry.title: Subject of Service now ticket created from Cloud ALM Job Monitoring does not comes with Job name
entry.content:
After configuring job monitoring in Cloud ALM and creating the integration with Service Now in order to get automatic ticket for created alerts the tickets are created in Service Now, but the ticket subject doesn't contain the Job name.
How to create a ticket with the Job Name in the subject?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Subject of Service now ticket created from Cloud ALM...
entry.content:
Hello, 

The built-in mapping for Service now does not include the job name in the subject of the ticket.
In order to include the required fields kindly review and adjust the mapping as per instructed in "Create Mapping" section of Integration with External Ticket Systems.
The available fields for usage are described in https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/apis/event-situations

In order to include the job name in the subject you may include one of the following fields:
-tags.subject -> Event name + Job name
-tags.NAME -> Job name

Best Regards

entry.title: Story Report for Goal Management
entry.content:
Hello,

I am creating story report for Goal Management, upon adding the template name, it is coming as blank in output, kindly suggest how to rectify it.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: What tips would you give an organisation planning to...
entry.content: Always try to see the bigger picture, try to push for clean core as the system which you are going to build will stay for longer run.

entry.title: Re: SAP MM
entry.content: This is custom message, and I believe you are performing FUT, in this case you need to connect with Tech team as validation is not maintained correctly.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere - Copy objects between Spaces within the s...
entry.content: Hi Hiri, seems the connection was not changed in the JSON. All occurrences need to be replaced. This works. Check what object is having the warning, is it data / replication flow or model? Cheers, Martin

entry.title: Re: Custom field and logics
entry.content:
Hello Fatima,

have you checked the customer migration object's documentation (custom field section)?

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/b251af240d9c41e9b9baf94603b9491a.html

the business context is assigned 

 

Business Context Structure

Master Data: Business Partner (BP_CUSTVEND1)General Data (S_CUST_GEN)
Customer Core View (CUSTOMER_GENERAL)General Data (S_CUST_GEN)
Customer company code core (CUST_COMPANYCODE)Company Data (S_CUST_COMPANY)
Partner Sales area core view (CUST_SALES)Sales Data (S_CUST_SALES_DATA)

Each business context is assigned to an API structure and that API structure is assigned to a sender structure within the migration object. There is no API structure existing for Key-user-extensibility support for tax data.
The business context Customerer Core View (Customer General) is assigned to S_CUST_GEN (General Data) structure. Fields within that business context are only visible in that structure. They cannot be made visible in another structure than the one mentioned within the documentation.

BR

Frank

entry.title: Re: data action to copy data by version and date from cla...
entry.content:
@Raj4 

Its difficult to provide a working code without model knowledge. But LINK syntax is something like below

DATA() = LINK([AccountModel], [d/Version] = "Plan01",  [d/Entity] = "REG0001",[d/Product] = "PRD0002", [d/Customer] = "CUST0001")

You can refer to the syntax here

Change Values in Advanced Formulas with DATA, RESULTLOOKUP, and LINK | SAP Help Portal 

 

Hope this helps

Nikhil

Additionally, the "Status" field under Basic User Information schema  is greyed out.

kindly suggest how to achieve it.

 

Thanks!

You need to switch to Hierarchy with Directory. That supports compounded keys

entry.title: Printing Adobe forms via local server or AWS
entry.content: Mass printing of invoices from Adobe, using a custom-developed program, is getting stuck on these invoice printing processes (approximately 12,000 per day). Do you recommend setting up a local server for Adobe or setting it up on AWS? Which is more efficient and secure?

entry.content:
Hi,

It is mentioned in the website that SAP UI5 version 1.71.69 will expire in Q3/2025. Will it expire before the start of Q3 or at the end of Q3/2025? When do we need to update the version? Can someone please tell me.

entry.title: Re: HTML5 Apps launched form SAP build workzone site are not opening
entry.content: There is no backend system involved. Its just static content

entry.title: Re: Printing Adobe forms via local server or AWS
entry.content:
Nog entirely sure if by AWS you mean hosting then (otherwise local) server on that hyperscaler, or doing it on BTP but a little over 15 euro a day is a steal.

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/serviceCatalog/forms-service-by-adobe?region=all&tab=service_plan

entry.title: Re: Issue with recording completions for multiple Content Objects in a single Item
entry.content: We are having the same issue and we are using SCORM 2004 4Ed.

entry.title: Re: Printing Adobe forms via local server or AWS
entry.content: Hi MatthiasL Thanks for the reply. When I refer to AWS, I mean installing it on the AWS server where we already have other servers. Today, we have a dedicated local server for Adobe services.I didn't understand, via BTP?

entry.title: Re: Adding Additional Quantity Fields in Account Based CO-PA
entry.content: I have an issue when running the report Market Segment - Actuals where it is not summing the amount in Additional Quantity 1 and only return "*" in the totals.  Any idea how to fix this?

entry.title: Re: SAP UI5 version 1.71.69 will expire in Q3/2025. Will...
entry.content:
It means the end of the quarter. So on October 1st 2025 you should have it updated already. If you don't do it and use WorkZone, all apps will break in DEV + TEST + PROD at the same day and time.

As patches usually last 1 years ideally you should upgrade them every six months or so.

entry.title: Re: Printing Adobe forms via local server or AWS
entry.content: SAP BTP has its own, hosted, Adobe Forms printing service. Basically enable it in BTP, link a RFC to the Cloud application and some other configuration, and you are printing all kinds of Adobe Forms by a specific SaaS application for SAP. More info on link above.

entry.title: How to set up a new start condition (WERKS) for the flexible workflow for purchase orders?
entry.content:
We need to create a new WORKFLOW start condition in SAP Public. Currently, we only have

 

Thank you,

entry.title: Re: My SAP Sapphire Orlando experience
entry.content:
Awesome post, Brian! Thank you for the warm introduction. I had the pleasure of attending my first SAP Sapphire this year, and your leadership and guidance onsite made a huge difference in helping me navigate the event and maximize the experience—for both myself and our customers.

I also had the opportunity to showcase SAP Sales and Service Cloud solutions at one of our CX booths, and I was struck by how energized and engaged our customers were—especially around the potential of AI to transform service operations. That enthusiasm really echoed what we’re seeing in the field: organizations are eager to streamline their service journeys, and they’re turning to SAP for intelligent, integrated solutions to make it happen.

The excitement around AI agents like the Case Classification Agent and Knowledge Creation Agent was palpable. I had several conversations that reinforced your key takeaway—AI and integration aren’t just trends; they’re becoming foundational to how enterprises deliver value.

Looking forward to continuing the conversation and learning from this community!

 

Brendan Del Biondo demonstrating key features of Sales Cloud at Sapphire 2025

entry.title: IDOC xsd import giving error in message mapping in SAP CPI iflow
entry.content:
Hi experts,
I am trying to upload the IDOC xsd (from PO ->IDOC ->xsd) but i am getting the error on attributes and also on idoc control segment field
Reference - IDOC xsd import error.png
 
I then came across the below post
CPI Message Mapping XSD Error
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/cpi-message-mapping-xsd-error/qaq-p/12048997
 
I tried to follow the steps as suggested -
Downloaded the XML Schema at http://www.w3.org/2001/xml.xsd and uploaded it as a resource to the iflow
the IDOC xsd does not have any reference and no import 
But tried it anyways which i think is the wrong location anyways and it gave error as expected -<xsd:schema xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" schemaLocation="xml.xsd"/>
 
I have searched and i am unable to find why an IDOC xsd generated from PO is not getting uploaded correctly
I can view it correctly and the xsd is valid
 
Please help me as i need to be able to upload the xsd for my message mapping

entry.title: Re: Which development installation should I choose?
entry.content: The only thing you need is the Crystal Reports Designer.

entry.title: Fiori App Controller Extension: Overrides not being triggered successfully
entry.content:
I'm trying to make a simple controller extension, which would hook onto a method when user clicks a button. The name of this method is "onClickActionC_MaintenanceJobWorkItemTPHeader1."

I've tried the following code, and several variations, trying to get a simple console.log message, but I can't get any of the methods below to trigger. Is there something I'm missing in order to get any of the below methods to trigger after their respective base controller methods are called?

 

sap.ui.define(
    [
        'sap/ui/core/mvc/ControllerExtension',
        'sap/ui/core/mvc/OverrideExecution'
    ],

   
    function (
        ControllerExtension,
        OverrideExecution //,Dialog, Button, mobileLibrary
       
    ) {
        console.log("testing 1");
        "use strict";
       
        return ControllerExtension.extend("customer.zperform.maintenance.jobs.PopupMessage", {
            metadata: {
                methods: {
                    onclick: {
                        public: true,
                        final: false,
                        overrideExecution: "After",
                    },

                    onmousedown: {
                        public: true,
                        final: false,
                        overrideExecution: "After",
                    },

                    onClickActionC_MaintenanceJobWorkItemTPHeader1: {
                        public: true,
                        final: false,
                        overrideExecution: "After",
                    },
                }
            },
            override: {
                onclick: function (oEvent) {
                    console.log("testing 2");
                },

                onmousedown: function (oEvent) {
                    console.log("testing 2");
                },
               
                onClickActionC_MaintenanceJobWorkItemTPHeader1: function (oEvent) {
                    console.log("testing 2");
                }
            }
        });
    }
)

entry.title: Re: SAP-консультант: хто це та як ним стати
entry.content:
Можливо, це має бути в

https://community.sap.com/t5/kyiv-events/eb-p/kyivoccasion-board

?

entry.title: Re: Fiori App Controller Extension: Overrides not being triggered successfully
entry.content: Please post the manifest as well

entry.title: Why purchasing group is created in material management configuration section instead of org. stru.
entry.content:
Hello All, 

As you all know that purchasing group is an organizational unit of the material management and also a floating org. unit, but when we create it we go to material management configuration path section instead of  organization structure (definition). The only answer I have thought about is because it has not assignment to any other org. unit. I want to make sure about this. I would appreciate anyone who can clarify this and thank you in advance. 
MM (Materials Management) 
MM Purchasing 
SAP Supply Chain Management 

SAP Community

entry.title: Physical quantity and available quantity in EWM
entry.content:
Hi All,

Can someone explain what is the difference between physical quantity and available quantity in SAP EWM?

entry.title: Re: Which development installation should I choose?
entry.content: I'm shocked. I won't even need the 64-bit runtime to be installed? Thank you for the response.

entry.title: Re: Which development installation should I choose?
entry.content: I hit enter too fast. I also meant to clarify that we have a web application which uses the .rpt files with a sql server database. I was under the impression I needed at least the runtime installation for my application to be able to open the reports.

entry.title: Re: Blog it forward - third time's a charm
entry.content: @TammyPowlas I enjoyed your blog a lot. I watched your video talk in full too. Your work is inspiring and I think that we need a redo community talk with you, @DiegoDora , and many more this year! Thanks for sharing!

entry.title: Re: Can the flavor builder role be limited in scope?
entry.content:
Hi Cristin,

Yes. You can create your own role(s) as described here.

entry.title: Re: Can the flavor builder role be limited in scope?
entry.content: Thank you!
